summary:
Maternal mortality disparities in low- and middle-income countries arise primarily from structural health system issues such as delayed diagnosis and inadequate data collection, not simply lack of funding. Structured audits remain effective in identifying preventable causes and driving improvement in maternal care regardless of setting.  

Final Answer: B
